text.title: Client Handover Package Core Rules
text.summary: Seven testable rules every handover package must satisfy.

rule.statement: Package MUST contain 7 sections: scope summary, runbook (start/stop/debug), architecture overview, ops surface (logs/metrics/alerts), credentials transfer log, open items with risk, 30-day support window terms.
rule.rationale: Missing any one of the 7 is the difference between a clean close and a 3-month ping queue.

rule.statement: Every credential in the transfer log records: name, vault path, rotation_date, acknowledged_by client (email + date). Untransferred / un-rotated credentials block the close.
rule.rationale: Credentials staying in the consultant's vault is a security incident waiting; rotation is the only durable proof of transfer.

rule.statement: Every open item lists: title, severity (high/medium/low), effort (S/M/L), blast radius (1/3/5), and recommended next step. "Misc cleanup" lists are rejected.
rule.rationale: Unspecified open items become "you said it was done" disputes.

rule.statement: The package names a successor (client-side engineer OR new consultant) by email. Generic "the team" successor is rejected.
rule.rationale: A named successor has a face for follow-up; a team alias does not.

rule.statement: Sign-off requires BOTH consultant and client signatures (emails + ISO dates). Single-side sign-off is rejected.
rule.rationale: Dual sign-off prevents "I never received the handover" disputes.

rule.statement: Document the 30-day support window: scope (bug-fixes vs new work), response SLA, contact channel, after-window terms. Vague "I'll be available" is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without explicit window terms, the engagement extends indefinitely.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (scope, credentials list, runbook draft, successor), execute: draft → transfer secrets → review with successor → dual sign-off → archive. Do not skip rotation evidence.
rule.rationale: Skipping rotation = security incident; skipping dual sign-off = invoice dispute risk.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) single-PR engagement, (b) client refuses successor identification — escalate, (c) existing handover record < 30 days, (d) system being decommissioned — use decommission methodology.
